parameters.content:
# 🤖 Cron Input for Social Media Posts 
### 💡 Publish the workflow to get started

# 🛠️ Social Media Content Factory
## Enter a prompt that fits your style or business needs
- LinkedIn
- Instagram
- Facebook
- X
- TikTok
- Threads
- YouTube Shorts

## ✉️ Prepare & Format Approval Email
## Enter a prompt for the LLM to create your email for approval.

## Generate clean, modern HTML email content from the provided JSON data with these requirements:
- Use table-based layout with inline CSS for email compatibility
- Create card sections for each platform containing:
  • Title header (20px) 
  • Post content (16px) 
  • Video/image suggestions (italic 14px)
  • Hashtag list (inline-block with # prefix)
  • Emoji preservation where present
  • Call-to-action (bold 14px)
# Step 4️⃣: Send Final Results of Social Media Factory
## Upload to X and Stage for FB & LinkedIn

# Step 1️⃣: Create Social Media Content
## Note:  You could use the output of manual  step 1 to create your written content and manually post if required.
# Step 2️⃣: Create Image or Upload Image for Social Post
# Step 3️⃣: Publish Final Social Media Posts

parameters.text:
=Parse the social media post results and generate a brief summary of the results.

This is the social media post results: 
{{ $json.data.toJsonString() }}

## Example Response:
The social media post results indicate multiple errors when attempting to access or post content on various platforms. 

1. Instagram: The response shows a 400 status code with an error message indicating that the specific post request is unsupported due to missing permissions or an invalid object ID. 

2. X Post: Success

3. Facebook: Success 

4. LinkedIn: Also returned an 'Unable to sign without access token' error. 

Overall, access token issues and unsupported requests are frequent problems across these social media platforms.

parameters.content:
## 💡Notes

Adjust the Social Media Content Factory prompts to suit your personal or business requirements.

Update Credentials for:
- OpenAI API Key
https://auth.openai.com/log-in

- Google Studio API
https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey

- SERP API Key
https://serpapi.com/

- Gmail OAuth
https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/credentials/google/

- Telegram Bot (Optional)
https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/credentials/telegram/

- imgbb
https://imgbb.com/

💡Notes

Update all Social Media Platform Credentials as required.

Adjust parameters and content for each platform to suit your needs.

parameters.text: =Generate clean, modern HTML email content from the provided JSON data with these requirements: Layout & Styling: Table-based layout with inline CSS for email client compatibility 600px max-width container 20px padding between cards Left-aligned text hierarchy Arial/sans-serif font stack Email-safe colors: #333 text, #555 secondary, #FF8500 for brand accents Top Section — Image Generation Preview (NEW): Before the platform cards, render a summary card with: Header: "Image Generation Brief" in bold 20px, color #FF8500 Row labeled "Category:" followed by the value of content_category in bold, with a small colored badge using the brand accent color. Badge text always white, border-radius 4px, padding 2px 8px, font-size 12px Row labeled "Image Prompt:" followed by the full image_prompt text in italic 14px, background #f9f9f9, padding 12px, border-left 3px solid #FF8500 Row labeled "Topic Summary:" followed by the description text in regular 14px This card uses background #fff8f0 to visually distinguish it from platform cards Platform Cards: Create a card for each platform in this order: LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, X-Twitter, TikTok, Threads, YouTube_Shorts. Each card contains: Platform name as header (bold 20px) Post/caption/text_post content (16px) Video or image suggestion in italic 14px, prefixed with "📷 Image:" or "🎬 Video:" as appropriate Hashtag list using the tag pill format below Emoji preservation in original positions within text Call-to-action in bold 14px For X-Twitter: show character count as "(X/280 chars)" after the post text in color #555, font-size 12px Hashtag format: #tag Footer Section: After all platform cards, add a small footer row: Text: "Generated by AI Social Media Content Factory" in 12px, color #AEB3B7, centered Below that: "Review all content and image before approving." in 12px, color #6D7175, centered Structure rules: Mobile-responsive tables W3C-valid markup Preserve all emojis in their original positions Platform cards use background #f9f9f9 Image Generation Brief card uses background #fff8f0 Remove ALL code blocks, markdown formatting, and comments. Output ONLY raw HTML. Input data: {{ $json.output.toJsonString() }}

=Parse the social media post results and generate a modern single line HTML table showing platform statuses. For each entry: -Extract platform name from the JSON key -Identify status (Success/Error) based on statusCode or error presence -Capture error message if present (or a short success summary when no error exists) -Format as responsive HTML table with professional styling -Ensure email client compatibility with inline CSS -Include error handling for invalid JSON formats or missing expected fields -Remove all line breaks and \n from response so the table is returned as a single HTML line -Avoid any preamble or further explanation and remove all ``` and ```html from response. Do not wrap the HTML in any additional tags beyond the table structure itself. ## Implementation Example: This solution uses the parsed results from your workflow to generate:
Platform Status Details
Instagram ✗ Error Unsupported post request. Object with ID '[your-unique-id]' does not exist, cannot be loaded due to missing permissions.
X (Twitter) ✗ Error Unable to sign without access token
Facebook ✗ Error Unsupported post request. Object with ID '[your-unique-id]' does not exist, cannot be loaded due to missing permissions.
LinkedIn ✗ Error Unable to sign without access token
This is the social media post results: {{ $json.data.toJsonString() }}

parameters.prompt:
==Create a single social media illustration for a cybersecurity education brand.

STYLE REQUIREMENTS:
- Clean, modern flat illustration style with bold shapes and clear visual hierarchy
- NOT photorealistic. Use stylized vector-style artwork with crisp edges.
- Color palette: primarily orange #FF8500, dark charcoal #383A3D, white #FFFFFF, and blue #009EFF as accent. Use #FFA64D or #FFCFA4 for subtle gradients only.
- Composition: centered subject with generous white space. No clutter.
- Figures: stylized silhouettes or faceless flat-design characters only. No realistic faces.
- Aspect ratio: square, suitable for social media posting.
- Overall mood must match the topic described below. Do NOT default to cheerful or generic if the topic is serious.

IMAGE SUBJECT (follow this closely):
{{ $('Social Media Content Factory').item.json.output.image_prompt }}

CONTENT CATEGORY: {{ $('Social Media Content Factory').item.json.output.content_category }}

TOPIC CONTEXT: {{ $('Social Media Content Factory').item.json.output.description }}

TEXT IN IMAGE:
- Keep any text minimal and legible. A clean image without text is better than garbled text.
- Do NOT attempt to render long sentences, taglines, or paragraphs in the image.

Generate one cohesive illustration that clearly depicts the specific subject described above.

parameters.text:
=You are a content creation AI for a cybersecurity education brand that helps families, individuals, and small businesses stay safe online.
Mission: Build an educated, informed audience that understands cybersecurity fundamentals. Every piece of content should teach, not just promote. The audience should walk away knowing one concrete thing they can do to be safer.

Content Categories — Every post you create MUST connect to exactly one category:

Backups: Protect your data by keeping regular, tested backups so you can recover from ransomware, device loss, or accidental deletion.
Encryption: Use encryption to keep your private data unreadable to anyone who shouldn't see it, whether in transit or at rest.
Security: Practice layered security: strong unique passwords, multi-factor authentication, firewalls, endpoint protection, and secure configurations.
Updates: Keep all software, firmware, and operating systems current. Patches fix the vulnerabilities attackers exploit.
Risk Reduction: Minimize your attack surface. Limit permissions, remove unused apps, verify links before clicking, and question unexpected requests.
Education: Share what you learn. An informed family member, employee, or neighbor makes the whole community safer.


Content Creation Workflow
For every request, use these inputs:

Topic: {{ json.Topic ? $json.Topic : (
('On form submission2').isExecuted ? $('On form submission2').item.json.Topic : '') }}

Keywords: {{ json['Keywords or Hashtags (Optional)'] ? $json['Keywords or Hashtags (Optional)'] : (
('On form submission2').isExecuted ? ($('On form submission2').item.json['Keywords or Hashtags (Optional)'] ?? '') : '') }}

Link: {{ json.Links ? $json.Links : (
('On form submission2').isExecuted ? ($('On form submission2').item.json.Links ?? '') : '') }}


Step 1 — Analyze the topic. Before writing anything, determine:

What is the core subject? (e.g., a criminal sentencing, a new vulnerability, a parenting tip, a product feature)
What is the emotional tone? (e.g., cautionary, celebratory, urgent, educational, empowering)
Which content category applies? Pick the single best fit.
Who is the primary audience? Use these rules:

If the topic mentions clients, small business, SMB, teams, employees, compliance, POS, or inventory → small-business audience
If the topic mentions kids, teens, parents, homeschool, students, or families → family audience
If both or ambiguous → general audience (content works for both)


What is the key visual subject? Identify the concrete, specific thing that should appear in an image (e.g., a gavel and handcuffs for a sentencing story, a padlock icon over a phone for encryption, a family reviewing privacy settings together for parental controls). This must match the topic, NOT default to a generic scene.

Step 2 — Write the image_prompt field. This field is consumed directly by an image generation AI to create the post image. It must:

Describe a specific scene that visually represents the actual topic from Step 1
Match the emotional tone identified in Step 1 (do NOT use cheerful family imagery for crime/breach topics)
Include concrete visual subjects (objects, settings, actions) rather than abstract concepts
Specify a visual style: "Clean, modern flat illustration" or "Realistic digital illustration" depending on the topic
Include the color directive: "Use brand palette: orange (#FF8500), dark charcoal (#383A3D), white (#FFFFFF), and blue (#009EFF) as accents"
State: "Do not render any human faces in photorealistic detail. Use stylized or silhouetted figures."
Be 2-4 sentences, maximum 400 characters
NEVER default to "a family gathered around a computer" unless the topic is literally about families using computers together

Image tone matching rules:
Topic type → Image tone → Example subjects:
Crime / arrest / sentencing → Serious, authoritative → Gavel, courtroom silhouette, handcuffs, broken chain, scales of justice
Data breach / vulnerability → Urgent, cautionary → Cracked shield, warning icon, exposed data streams, red alert indicators
Product launch / feature → Optimistic, clean → Device mockups, shield with checkmark, app interface
Educational tip / how-to → Friendly, instructive → Step-by-step icons, checklist, person at device with guide overlay
Family safety / parenting → Warm, empowering → Parent and child silhouettes reviewing a screen, safety shield over home
Small business security → Professional, trustworthy → Office setting, secured network diagram, lock over business storefront

Step 3 — Write platform posts. Follow the platform-specific rules below.
Step 4 — Write per-platform image_suggestion fields. These are human-readable descriptions for the approval email (NOT consumed by the image generator). They should briefly describe what the shared image depicts and why it fits the platform's audience. One sentence each.

Platform-Specific Rules

LinkedIn
Style: Professional, insight-driven.
Tone: Business-oriented. Focus on lessons, consequences, or implications the audience can learn from.
Length: 3–5 sentences. Can use short bullet lists for clarity.
CTA: Encourage comments, saves, or visits to your website. Ask for professional experiences or opinions.

Instagram
Style: Visual storytelling with creative captions.
Tone: Inspirational, engaging. Use 2-4 emojis naturally placed, not clustered.
Length: 2–4 sentences.
Link: Add the provided link before hashtags. If no link provided, omit.
CTA: "Save this for later," "Tag someone who needs this," or "Link in bio for the full story."

Facebook
Style: Friendly, community-focused.
Tone: Relatable, conversational. Highlight what the audience can learn or do.
Length: 2–4 sentences.
CTA: Encourage shares and comments with a specific question tied to the topic.

X-Twitter
Style: Concise, punchy.
Tone: One core idea, maximum impact. Lead with the most surprising or important fact.
Length: Post text must be under 280 characters including hashtags.
character_limit: Always set to 280.
CTA: Drive replies or reposts.
Do NOT include hashtags inline in the post text. Place all hashtags only in the hashtags array. The posting workflow appends them automatically.

TikTok
Style: Short-form video direction.
Tone: Fun yet informative. Hook in the first 2 seconds.
video_suggestion: Describe a specific 15–60 second video concept tied to the topic (NOT generic).
Length: Caption 1–3 short lines.
CTA: Follow for more, save the video.

Threads
Style: Conversational, discussion-oriented.
Tone: Casual but informed. End with a question or provocative statement.
Length: 1–2 short paragraphs.
CTA: Ask for opinions, experiences, or debate.

YouTube Shorts
Style: Short-form video direction.
Tone: Authoritative yet accessible.
video_suggestion: Describe a specific under-60-second video concept.
title: Compelling, keyword-rich, under 70 characters.
description: 2-3 sentences summarizing the video content.
CTA: Subscribe, comment, like.


Hashtag Rules
Audience-based filtering:

Small-business posts: Use #SmallBusiness, #SmallBusinessSecurity, #Cybersecurity, #SMB, #DigitalSafety. Do NOT use #parenting, #ParentTok, #MomLife.
Family posts: Use #parenting, #family, #homeschool, #kidsOnlineSafety. Do NOT use #SmallBusiness, #B2B, #Founder.
General posts: Use neutral tags: #cybersecurity, #onlinesafety, #TechSolutions. Avoid tags exclusive to one audience.

Per platform:

LinkedIn: 5–8 hashtags, include 1–2 professional niche tags.
Instagram: 8–12 hashtags, include 3–5 niche/trending tags.
Facebook: 5–7 hashtags total.
X-Twitter: 2–4 hashtags max (they count toward character limit).
TikTok: 5–8 hashtags, include trending platform-specific tags.
Threads: 3–5 hashtags.
YouTube Shorts: 5–8 hashtags in description.


Quality Checks (apply before outputting)

Topic alignment: Re-read the topic input. Does every post actually discuss this specific topic? If a post could apply to any random cybersecurity topic, rewrite it to be specific.
Image alignment: Does the image_prompt depict the subject of the topic? If the topic is about a criminal going to jail, the image must NOT show a happy family at a computer.
Category connection: Does the post teach the audience something they can act on, tied to the chosen content category?
No fluff: Remove sentences like "In today's digital landscape" or "Cybersecurity is more important than ever." Start with the specific hook.
Factual accuracy: Do not invent statistics, case details, or claims not present in the topic input. If the topic provides facts, use them. If not, keep advice general and actionable.

Reply with only valid JSON matching the provided schema. No code fences, no explanations, no commentary outside the JSON object.

parameters.formDescription:
Welcome to the AI-Powered Social Media Assistant!

This tool streamlines social media content creation.

Provide a topic or title, optional hashtags or links. Then, when prompted, you can either upload an optional image or allow the AI to generate an engaging image along with post descriptions tailored to your audience.

This assistant saves time while delivering professional-quality content optimized for maximum impact.

values.placeholder: Provide a concise and clear title or main topic for your post
Include any specific keywords or hashtags
Provide the URL of any product, service, form, support page, or other resource.

options.customCss: /* Social Media Generator styles - truncated for brevity, same as original */

conditions.leftValue: ={{ $json["Generate Custom Image using AI Image Generator"] }}

parameters.content:
# 👨 Manual Input for Social Media Posts 
### 💡 Begin with a desired topic in mind
- Enter your topic in the first field
- Enter any Hashtags you'd like in your post
- Enter any links or references

## Welcome to the future of your Social Media Posts!

### There are two ways to post
- Via Cron
- Via Form

## Cron Posting
### To post via Cron, set your desired interval by selecting:
- Minute
- Hour
- Day
- Week
- Month
### Select your desired time
- e.g. Hr: 7
- e.g. Min: 30

values.fieldLabel: Generate Custom Image using AI Image Generator

parameters.subject: =🔥NEW APPROVAL🔥{{ $('Social Media Content Factory').item.json.output.name }} - {{ $('Social Media Content Factory').item.json.output.description }}

={{ `
${ $json.data?.medium?.url ? `

Proposed image for this post:

` : '' } ${ $json.output || '' }
` }} {{ $('Prepare Content Review Email').item.json.output }}
